At first, the best candidate for the job was the Nameless. But unexpectedly, that poor chap Akivili ended up kicking the bucket, and the rest of the gang disappeared without a trace soon after... Without this first-choice option available, a traveling merchant was the most reliable — and so they invited me.
说到底，这宇宙中还有多少人配得上「无名客」的称呼呢？
After all, how many people in this universe are worthy of the "Nameless" title?
我们星穹列车应该可以？
What about us aboard the Astral Express?
等会儿，你是…星穹列车的成员？它现在情况如何了？
Hang on, are you... a member of the Astral Express? How are things there right now?
你向科琳概述了星穹列车一路的经历。
You share with Colleen an overview of your experiences aboard the Astral Express.
…真令人感慨，没想到这么多琥珀纪过去了，居然还有人行走在正统的「开拓」命途上。
...That's really touching. I didn't expect that after so many Amber Eras there would still be people walking the Path of Trailblaze.
…咳，失礼了。既然你已经为我讲了这么多经历，作为回报，我也该给你讲讲我的。
...*ahem* I'm sorry. Since you've told me so much about your experiences, I should offer up the same as thanks and tell you a little about myself.
那么，你喜欢听什么样的故事？
So then, what kind of stories do you like best?
哟，你又来了，这次有什么打算吗？
Yo, you're back. What's the plan this time?
I'd like to hear about your experiences.
好呀，科琳船长的故事多到说不完。你喜欢听什么样的故事？
Alright, Captain Colleen's got many a story. What kind of stories do you like best?
有一次，我受博识学会委托，前去回收位于「新伊甸」大黑洞附近的一个侦测信标。报酬非常优厚，所以我没多想就同意了。

There was this one time on an assignment from the Intelligentsia Guild, when we were retrieving a probe beacon from nearby this mega black hole called the New Eden. The pay was so fat that we agreed without thinking too much about it.
一去才发现，信标早就不在原来的位置了。某个意外让它脱离了固定点，正朝着黑洞所在的方向一路飘去，很快就会越过事件视界。

Upon arrival, we discovered that the beacon wasn't in its original spot. An accident had dragged it away from its fixed location, and it was drifting toward the black hole — soon to cross over the event horizon.
越过事件视界就相当于扎进巨真蛰虫的胃袋——有去无回。所以我必须在信标彻底跌入黑洞前把它截住…这真是我船长生涯中最棘手的挑战。

Crossing the event horizon is like plunging into the stomach of a Giant Sting: There's no coming back from it. So I had to intercept the beacon before it fell completely into the black hole... This was the most difficult challenge I ever encountered in my career as a captain.
要知道，事件视界之外也是有引力的，而且越靠近中心、引力就越强。我那艘船的发动机不是尖货，没法保证取到信标后还能出来……

Y'know what, there is gravity outside the event horizon, and it gets stronger the closer you get to the center. My ship's engine wasn't the best, and there was no guarantee that we could even get out once we'd nabbed the beacon...
…好在信标的前进方向上有不少金属碎片。我可以从船上发射一个东西，与那些碎片碰撞、反弹，从而给信标施加一个往回来的力……
...Luckily there were lots of metal scraps in the beacon's path. I could launch something from the ship to collide with the scraps and ricochet, thereby exerting a returning force onto the beacon...
这原理解释起来可就复杂了…知道{RUBY_B#台球}撞洞球{RUBY_E#}吗？就跟那个差不多，但我要打的是有史以来最精妙，最传奇的一杆！

This principle is quite hard to explain... Do you know {RUBY_B#Billiards}billiards{RUBY_E#}? It's just like that, but the shot I was gonna make would be the most exquisite, legendary shot in all history!
是啊，入射角度必须精确，发射力度必须适中；任何失误都会破坏碎片的排列，根本没有重来的余地。
Yeah, the angle of incidence must be exact with moderate force only; any mistakes would destroy the arrangement of the scraps. There was no room for a mulligan here.
不，要真那样的话就算不上惊险了……
No, if it were that alone, it wouldn't count as being thrilling...
是不是很好奇，为什么这样的故事称得上惊险呢？别急，我来告诉你……
Aren't you curious as to why this story isn't considered thrilling, though? Don't worry. I'll fill you in...
那一杆打得并不好。飞船发出的物体没有按照预想回弹，而是像没头苍蝇一样乱撞一气，反过来加速了信标的沉没。
The shot was no good. The missiles did not rebound as expected, but instead smashed around like a headless fly — which then accelerated the sinking of the beacon.
其实这个时候，我还是可以开足马力追上信标的。但那风险实在太大，不值得。
At this point, I could actually still speed in to catch up with the beacon. But the risk was too great to be worth it.
所以我调转船头，打算直接驶离这片星系…结果迎面撞上了一支泯灭帮的舰队。那群疯狗，追的那叫一个紧！我都怀疑他们是学会派来追杀我的。

And so I turned around the spaceship and planned to jet straight out of that star system... but ended up running straight into an Annihilation Gang fleet. Those crazy mutts were doing all they could to chase me down! I even suspected that perhaps the guild had sent them to hunt me down.
我的飞船在围攻中被华丽地炸成了碎片，我本人则借着爆风登上了泯灭帮的战舰。
My ship was blown into magnificent smithereens during the assault, and I used the force of the explosion to board the Annihilation Gang's battleship.
战斗过程暂且省略。最终，我成功制服了全部贼人，开着抢来的战舰一脚油门驶离了星系——这是最刺激的部分。

I won't go into details for now about the battle. In the end I managed to defeat all the bad guys, then put the pedal to the metal to pilot this stolen warship straight out of the star system — now THAT was the most thrilling part of the story.
呃…这里面涉及到很多暴力细节。我看你年纪轻轻，还是少听点不健康的内容为好。
Well... there was lots of violence in the details. I can tell you're young, though, so don't want to expose you to all that.
…总之，动用你的想象力吧。你不是个开拓者吗？那就不该被现实束缚了思维！
...Anyways, use your imagination! Aren't you a Trailblazer? If so, you shouldn't restrain your thinking to just reality!
是吧？大名鼎鼎的巡海游侠也做不到更好了。而这只是我传奇生涯中的一个小小注脚。
Right? Not even the illustrious Galaxy Rangers could do any better. And this is but one small, micro footnote in the annals of my legendary career.
有兴趣的话，之后再给你讲点其他的动作戏…等我花点时间编下细节。
If you're interested, I can spin a few more action stories for you later... just let me come up with some of the details first.
呃，你就没有点…感想什么的？
Er, don't you have any... thoughts?
算了，这个故事确实烂到蹩脚…忘了它吧。
Whatever, this story is terrible and hacky... just forget it.
Make it a little more romantic.
浪漫一点的…那就说说小行星带的事吧。
More romantic... Then let's talk about the incident in the asteroid belt.
有一次，我开着飞船在宇宙中闲逛，阴差阳错地来到了一个不存在于星图上的地方：一片遍布小行星的太空垃圾场。
One time, I was piloting a spaceship and roaming the stars, when I accidentally came to a place that wasn't on the astral charts: A space junkyard filled with asteroids.
这些偏僻星区的数据可珍贵了。只要把坐标上报给公司，我就能拿到一大笔信用点奖励。
The data in these remote star systems is priceless. I could get huge credit rewards for reporting the coordinates to IPC.
不过在那之前，我得先把周围仔细检查一遍——这么简单的钱却一直没人赚，背后肯定有鬼。
But before that, I had to carefully check our vicinity — this gig was all too easy, and there had to be something going on behind the scenes.
这地方障碍物太多，舰载仪器除了发出恼人的警报外什么忙都帮不上。没办法，我只能用肉眼观察，手动操控飞船。

There were so many obstacles in this place that the ship's instruments kept tripping alarms everywhere, rendering it useless. I had no choice but to make observations by dead reckoning, and manually piloted the spaceship.
就在我聚精会神、眼观六路的时候，一个张牙舞爪的外星人忽然飘到了驾驶窗前……
Just as I was focusing all my attentions on the six directions around me, the snarling teeth and claws of this alien suddenly floated into sight...
是啊！不过我并没有被吓到，我的大心脏可是比钻石都坚固——即便这怪物长着两对眼睛，皮肤比伊须磨人都光滑，我内心也毫无惧意。
Yeah! But I wasn't scared. My heart is tougher than diamond — despite this monster having two pairs of eyes and skin smoother than an Insumousu local. There was no dread in my heart.
我仔细一看，发现那家伙身上的皮肤干瘪不堪，似乎已经死了…看它的样子，在宇宙中生存应该没什么问题才对。
Upon closer look, I realized that this guy's skin was all dried up as though dead... Judging by its appearance, it should have no problem whatsoever surviving in the universe.
两对眼睛、皮肤光滑，有点像长了手脚的鱼——看它的样子，在宇宙中生存也没什么问题。
Two pairs of eyes and smooth skin, almost like a fish with arms and legs — judging by its appearance, it should have no problem whatsoever surviving in the universe.
后来我仔细一看，才发现那家伙已经死去多时了。
Upon closer look, I realized that this guy was dead, and had been so for some time.
我仔细一看，才发现那家伙已经死了。它长得像是有手脚的鱼，外表似乎很适合在宇宙中生存。
Upon closer look, I realized that this guy was dead. It looked like a fish with arms and legs, and its exterior seemed as though it would have no problem surviving out in the universe.
我从没在智库里见过类似的生物，这意味着它的种族要么尚未被发现，要么…已经灭亡。
I've never seen anything like it in the data bank, which means it's either an undiscovered species, or... extinct.
后来我又对这片星区做了些勘探，发现那些小行星并非自然形成。它们是一场爆炸产生的碎片，原本来自同一颗星球。
Later on, I did some digging around this star system and discovered those asteroids were not formed naturally. They were fragments from an explosion, and were all sourced from the same planet.
计算机解析了样本成分，测得碎片诞生于十多个琥珀纪前——那个时期发生了一件大事，你应该知道。
The computer analyzed the sample composition, and found that the fragments were formed over <unbreak>10</unbreak> Amber Eras ago — a huge event happened back then, which you should know about.
是星核——「万界之癌」就是自那时开始侵害诸界的。
It was a stellaron — the Cancer of All Worlds that set out on its invasion of all worlds thereafter.
没错，就是从那时起，「万界之癌」开始侵害诸界。
That's right. It was from that moment forth that the Cancer of All Worlds started its invasion across all worlds.
毁于星核的文明并不少见。最初我也没把这事放在心上，打算上报完坐标就走——直到我发现了这些小行星表面的符号。

Civilizations destroyed by stellarons are not altogether uncommon. At first, I didn't really take it seriously, and was planning on warping out of there as soon as I'd reported the coordinates — that is, until I discovered those symbols on the surface of the asteroids.
那真是十分壮观的场面，每颗小行星上都如纹身般镌刻着密集的块状痕迹。这些痕迹有深有浅、形状各异，无疑是经由人力开凿出来的，仿佛…某种文字。

It really was a magnificent sight, with these blocks of dense scratches etched onto each asteroid like a tattoo. There were both deep and shallow markings, in all kinds of different shapes, which undoubtedly were carved out by someone as if... they were glyphs from some civilization.
这幅奇景勾起了我的兴趣——哪个无名客能拒绝探险的邀请呢？
The sight piqued my interest — which of the Nameless could ever refuse such an invitation to explore?
于是我继续向垃圾场中央行驶，一路上尽是大大小小的刻字石，以及…修建在小行星上的房子。
So I continued on into the center of the trash dump, passing blocks with glyphs carved into them along the way as well as... little houses built onto the asteroids.
这些房子看起来像铁皮拼成的蜂巢，结构简单但数量繁多，显然是人工建造的——这里曾经住着一整个聚落！
These houses looked like little honeycombs woven out of tin foil. Simple in structure and numbering in the hundreds, they were clearly artificially built — an entire settlement once lived here!
我挨个检查那些住房，见到了更多外星人的尸体。房间内完全没有生存物资，除了简单的石镐外也几乎没有工具。
I checked over the houses but only found more alien corpses. There were no supplies in any of the buildings, and almost no tools at all save for a crude pickax.
可以想象，他们是这颗星球的幸存者，不知用什么手段逃离了灭顶之灾，却注定因资源耗尽走向死亡。
It's possible that they are the survivors of this planet, escaping utter catastrophe through some means but doomed to ultimately die due to exhaustion of resources.
在生命的最后时刻到来前，他们将至关重要的信息——可能是史书，也可能是对星核的研究——以文字形式{RUBY_B#记忆}记录{RUBY_E#}在了这些小行星表面。

Before the final moments of their lives, they {RUBY_B#memories}recorded{RUBY_E#} vital information — maybe histories, or research into Stellarons — in the form of glyphs carved into the surfaces of these asteroids.
这一工作实在太繁琐，太漫长…究竟是怎样坚定的意志在支撑他们做完这一切？我不知道，但他们的成果无疑铸就了母星文明的丰碑。

This work looks truly tedious and boring... What kind of firm resolve supported their completion? I don't know, but their achievements have no doubt left a monument for their home planet and civilization.
可惜联觉信标不能解读文字，漫长的无规则运动也早已破坏了这些小行星的顺序，不然我一定要读一读，看看究竟是什么内容能让他们在死亡面前保持团结。

It's a shame that the Synesthesia Beacon could not decrypt text, and long years of irregular movements have long since destroyed the sequence these asteroids were supposed to be read in. Otherwise, I would have read it — to see what it was that kept them going as a team on the brink of death.
最终，我按计划把坐标发送给公司，然后一脚油门离开了这里——应知晓的故事我已铭记，这些密文还是留给专业人士来伤脑筋吧。

In the end, I sent off the coordinates to IPC as planned, then put the pedal to the metal to hightail it out of there — I'd learned the lesson I was supposed to, and the secret of the glyphs should be left to professionals.
Sounds like it'll become a tourist destination...
也许吧，不过相比之下，我更不希望这里的故事被永远埋没。
Maybe. But in comparison, I'd rather this story wasn't buried forever.
…那样艰苦的努力却不能为人所知，未免太可惜了些。
...It would be a great pity for such hard work to go unrecognized.
Did IPC pay out a bonus?
没有。原本我确实想着要赚公司一笔，但见识了那样壮观的景象后，钱已经无足轻重了。
They sure did. I had been focused on making a score, but after witnessing such a grand sight, money no longer seemed to matter.
而且以公司的作风，我这个第一发现人肯定要被盘问个不停；不如早点溜走，好歹能捞着个清静。
Plus, based on the IPC's style, I knew I'd face endless interrogations as the person who made first contact. I'd be far better slipping off and getting some peace and quiet.
话说回来，这个故事够浪漫吗？
Anyhow, was that story romantic enough for you?
There's a kind of majestic beauty to it.
是吧？相比于爱情，我更喜欢这种不寻常的浪漫。
Right? I prefer this kind of unusual romance over traditional love.
银河中有亿万颗恒星，在如此广大的尺度上，二人间的执念难免显得渺小。
There are hundreds of millions of stars in the cosmos. On such a vast scale, this mutual infatuation between two people seems inevitably insignificant.
总之，你能喜欢它真是再好不过了。
Anyway, it's great that you liked it.
唉，果然用言语描述出来还是差了些味道。
*sigh* So it seems like you lose some of the magic when you put it into words.
有机会的话，你真该亲眼见识下那副景象…也许可以把它做成梦泡？
If you get the chance, you really should try and see that sight with your own eyes... Maybe you could make it into a dream bubble?
哈哈，那确实也是一种浪漫，不过我更喜欢这种…不寻常的浪漫。
Haha, that's indeed one kind of romantic ideal, but I prefer this kind of... unusual romance.
毕竟，二人的执念只是银河中的万千联系之一。宇宙很精彩，不是吗？
After all, their infatuation is just one of uncountable connections throughout the cosmos. The universe is truly wonderful, isn't it?
嗯…一般来说，这片宇宙发生的一切都只是巧合的堆砌，人类很难从中学到什么道理。
Well... generally speaking, everything that happens in this universe is just a pile of coincidences stacked up, and it's difficult for humans to learn anything from that.
不过，我确实有个符合你要求的故事。
However, I do have a story that fits the bill.
大概十个琥珀纪前，星际间还流传着「机械海盗王」韦兰的臭名……
About <unbreak>10</unbreak> Amber Eras or so back, the notorious "Mechanical Pirate King" Wyland roamed the galaxies...
这位贼人年轻时几乎把自己身上的全部零件都换成了义体，在星区内兴风作乱，犯罪无数，头上顶着的悬赏足以买下一整颗星球。

When this pirate was young, he switched out almost all of his body parts with prosthetics, wreaking havoc throughout star zones and committing countless crimes. The bounty on his head was enough to buy a whole planet of your own.
等年老后，韦兰的脑子已经被过多的改造压垮。疼痛与幻觉每天折磨着他，把他变成了狂躁易怒又神经兮兮的…怪物。
In his old age, Wyland's mind became overwhelmed by excessive mods. Pain and hallucinations tortured him daily, twisting him into a mad, irritable, neurotic... monster.
曾经忠诚的部下要么离开了他，要么被他残忍地折磨。就在海盗团即将分崩离析时，一位戴面具的访客找上韦兰，说虚空鲸的大脑提取液可以缓解这种不良反应。

His once-loyal crew either deserted him or were cruelly tortured by him. Just as this band of pirates was about to fall apart, a masked visitor came to Wyland telling him that an extract from the brain of the void song whale could relieve his adverse reactions.
走投无路的韦兰自然不会放过任何一根救命稻草，对虚空鲸的屠杀自此拉开序幕……
At his wits' end, Wyland could not help but clutch to any last straw that might save his life. And so began the massacre of the void song whales...
为保证猎杀效率，韦兰将自己的神经连进了舰桥终端，从而对整艘战舰如臂指使。代价就是，一旦失去虚空鲸提取液的供给，他就会在数小时内器官衰竭，最后死去。

Wyland hooked his nervous system straight into the bridge terminal for maximum hunting efficiency, so that commanding the entire battleship was like moving a limb. The cost was that once he lost his supply of whale extract, he would experience organ failure and perish soon after.
那是一段疯狂的日子，每天都有鲸群在炮火中丧生。就连「泯灭帮」都被韦兰的雄心折服，自愿加入这场狩猎——净瞎凑热闹。
Those were crazy days, with whales perishing in the cannon fire every day. Even the Annihilation Gang was won over by Wyland's ambition, willingly joining this hunt — just for the thrill of it.
多次警告无果后，星际和平公司找上了巡海游侠，提议联手铲除这个作恶已久的祸害。最后，双方各自派出精锐战力，计划对韦兰的旗舰发动一次斩首行动。

After multiple unheeded warnings, the Interastral Peace Corporation reached out to the Galaxy Rangers about building an alliance to wipe out this enduring scourge. In the end, both sides contributed crack troops and planned to launch an operation to lop the head off Wyland's main battleship.
谁说不是呢？但即便这样豪华的阵容，也远远称不上胜券在握。
Who said it wasn't? But even with such a luxuriant lineup, victory was far from guaranteed.
不要以为斩首很容易。战舰的雷达就是韦兰的双眼，战舰的火炮则是他手臂的延伸。想要登上这样一艘怪物，没有过硬的驾驶技术可办不到。
Don't think a beheading operation is easy. The battleship's radar was Wyland's eyes. Its cannons were extensions of his arms. Boarding such a monster as this requires perfect mastery of piloting.
好在我也参与了那场战斗…以运输舰驾驶员的身份。
Luckily, I also participated in that battle... as the pilot of the transport vessel.
当我们打开舰长室的大门时，眼前只有空空如也的玻璃缸和杂乱丢弃的输液管——其中最大的那根链接着韦兰的后背……
When we opened the door to the captain's quarters, all we could see was an empty glass tank and scattered drips hanging about the place — the largest of which was hooked up to Wyland's back...
韦兰趴在地上，像条脱水的鱼一样，死了。
Wyland lay on the ground, like a fish out of water — dead.
不，那样就没有教育意义了。
No, that isn't really very educational.
当时我们也很疑惑。本以为会有一场惊世大战，没想到一弹未发就跳到了收尸阶段。
We were puzzled at the time too. We thought there would be this ferocious battle, but it shifted straight to the cleanup stage without a shot being fired.
后来我们得知：早在这场战斗发生前，韦兰就把自己手头的提取液全卖掉了，买家是一位出价颇高的面具人。
Later on, we learned that Wyland had sold off all the extract he had well before the battle to a masked buyer who paid very good money for it.
他原以为缸里的存货足够挺到下一次狩猎，结果找了几周都没发现猎物——根据博识学会的统计，那片星区早就没有活着的虚空鲸了。

He thought the supplies in the tank would last until the next hunt, but could find no prey after weeks of searching. According to the statistics from the Intelligentsia Guild, there haven't been any living void song whales in that sector for a long time.
所以，当联军打上门时，韦兰已经因义体的排异反应而死去多时……
So when the righteous alliance attacked, Wyland had already been dead for some time due to graft-host rejection...
直到生命的最后一刻，他还在试图用嘴去吸输液管，好弄出几滴残留的提取液来。
He was still trying to use his mouth to suck the last few drops of extract out of the tubes during his final moments.
是啊，也很有{RUBY_B#「欢愉」}乐子{RUBY_E#}。为祸一方的机械海盗王，最后落得个这样的死法。
Yes, truly an {RUBY_B#"Elation"}enjoyable{RUBY_E#} moment. The Mechanical Pirate King, a menace of the era, meeting his end like this.
最能打的韦兰死了，其他人自然作鸟兽散。不过公司和游侠还是让他们得到了应有的惩罚。
Wyland was the best fighter and died. The rest of them naturally scattered to the solar winds. But IPC and Rangers still meted out to them the punishment they deserved.
唉，有机体总是喜欢把自己改造成机械，韦兰也不例外……
*sigh* Organic creatures love modding themselves into robots. Wyland was no exception...
可他从来都没能摆脱人性中的贪欲，直到最后，滑稽地死在了管子拼成的王座里。
But he was never able to rid himself of human greed, until in the end he died farcically upon a throne made of twisted tubes.
我相信每个人都能从他的经历中学到不同的东西。而我的看法嘛…
I trust everyone is able to learn something different from his experiences. And in my opinion...
如果已经有许多人想要杀你，就别让自己陷入众叛亲离的境地。
If you already have people who want your head, don't let it get to the point where you've alienated your own family.
What did the alien look like?
I'm not so good at history...
公司与巡海游侠合作，真少见…
The IPC and the Galaxy Rangers working together? That is rare...
那就一路平安吧——这是对无名客最好的祝福。
Then safe travels — the best wishes there could ever be for the Nameless.
I'd like to hear about your experiences.
好呀，科琳船长的故事多到说不完。你喜欢听什么样的故事？
Alright, Captain Colleen's got many a story. What kind of stories do you like best?
有一次，我受博识学会委托，前去回收位于「新伊甸」大黑洞附近的一个侦测信标。报酬非常优厚，所以我没多想就同意了。

There was this one time on an assignment from the Intelligentsia Guild, when we were retrieving a probe beacon from nearby this mega black hole called the New Eden. The pay was so fat that we agreed without thinking too much about it.
一去才发现，信标早就不在原来的位置了。某个意外让它脱离了固定点，正朝着黑洞所在的方向一路飘去，很快就会越过事件视界。

Upon arrival, we discovered that the beacon wasn't in its original spot. An accident had dragged it away from its fixed location, and it was drifting toward the black hole — soon to cross over the event horizon.
越过事件视界就相当于扎进巨真蛰虫的胃袋——有去无回。所以我必须在信标彻底跌入黑洞前把它截住…这真是我船长生涯中最棘手的挑战。

Crossing the event horizon is like plunging into the stomach of a Giant Sting: There's no coming back from it. So I had to intercept the beacon before it fell completely into the black hole... This was the most difficult challenge I ever encountered in my career as a captain.
要知道，事件视界之外也是有引力的，而且越靠近中心、引力就越强。我那艘船的发动机不是尖货，没法保证取到信标后还能出来……

Y'know what, there is gravity outside the event horizon, and it gets stronger the closer you get to the center. My ship's engine wasn't the best, and there was no guarantee that we could even get out once we'd nabbed the beacon...
…好在信标的前进方向上有不少金属碎片。我可以从船上发射一个东西，与那些碎片碰撞、反弹，从而给信标施加一个往回来的力……
...Luckily there were lots of metal scraps in the beacon's path. I could launch something from the ship to collide with the scraps and ricochet, thereby exerting a returning force onto the beacon...
这原理解释起来可就复杂了…知道{RUBY_B#台球}撞洞球{RUBY_E#}吗？就跟那个差不多，但我要打的是有史以来最精妙，最传奇的一杆！

This principle is quite hard to explain... Do you know {RUBY_B#Billiards}billiards{RUBY_E#}? It's just like that, but the shot I was gonna make would be the most exquisite, legendary shot in all history!
是啊，入射角度必须精确，发射力度必须适中；任何失误都会破坏碎片的排列，根本没有重来的余地。
Yeah, the angle of incidence must be exact with moderate force only; any mistakes would destroy the arrangement of the scraps. There was no room for a mulligan here.
不，要真那样的话就算不上惊险了……
No, if it were that alone, it wouldn't count as being thrilling...
是不是很好奇，为什么这样的故事称得上惊险呢？别急，我来告诉你……
Aren't you curious as to why this story isn't considered thrilling, though? Don't worry. I'll fill you in...
那一杆打得并不好。飞船发出的物体没有按照预想回弹，而是像没头苍蝇一样乱撞一气，反过来加速了信标的沉没。
The shot was no good. The missiles did not rebound as expected, but instead smashed around like a headless fly — which then accelerated the sinking of the beacon.
于是我连忙开足马力追了上去——当时真该调头离开的，或许是开拓精神作祟吧。
So I quickly chased after him at full speed. I really should have turned around and left at that point, but perhaps it was the Trailblazer inside me.
成功回收信标的同时，我的飞船也被黑洞的引力捕获，搁浅在了吸积盘上。那感觉就像身处湍急的水流，不管怎么努力都无法前进，稍一松懈又会被推着后退。

While safely retrieving the beacon, my ship was captured by the gravity well of the black hole, trapping me on the accretion disk. It felt like being in water rapids — no matter how hard you tried there was no way forward, and if you relaxed for a second, it would push you back.
靠着引擎的超负荷运转，我暂时保持了受力平衡；但等到引擎燃料耗尽后，飞船还是会不可避免地坠入深渊。
Overloading the engine, I maintained a temporary force balance. However, once my fuel burned up, the spaceship would inevitably tumble straight into the abyss.
就在这时，我想起了先贤说过的一句话：「想要离开某地，必须先抛弃某物」。
Just then, I found myself remembering that line from the sages: "If you wish to leave somewhere, you must first abandon something."
于是我执行分离程序，将飞船的整个货仓都向后发射出去，借着产生的反冲力脱离了旋涡。
And so I performed a separation procedure, launching the entire cargo cabin of the spaceship backwards to use the recoil generated to break away from the black hole.
还好当初没有彻底落入事件视界，不然就只有星神知道会怎样了……
Luckily we didn't end up crossing the event horizon, otherwise only the Aeons know what might have happened...
但这并不是胜利。逃出黑洞之后，我发现日子已经过去了好久，比想象中还要久……
But this was no victory. Outside the vicinity of the black hole, I discovered that a long time had passed — far longer than I could've imagined...
交还信标时，所有人都用诧异的目光看向我——原来的委托人早就老死了，是他徒弟的徒弟为我付了酬劳。
When I handed back the beacon, everyone looked at me as though I had two heads: The original manager for the task had long died. It was his apprentice's apprentice who paid me out.
可以这么说。毕竟单凭凡人的寿命，我可没机会活到现在。
That's one way of putting it. After all, based on the lifespan of mortals I shouldn't be alive right now.
至于这到底是机缘还是诅咒，恐怕只有时间能给出答案……
As for whether this is a blessing or a curse, I'm afraid only time can deliver an answer...
The Intelligentsia Guild keeps their promises...
哈哈，如果真能遇见从十几个琥珀纪前来的人，我也很乐意付他钱——还有比这更酷的事了吗？
Haha, if I really could meet someone from dozens of Amber Eras ago, I'd be happy to pay them money — could there be anything cooler than this?
毕竟，可不是每个凡人都有机会活那么久。
After all, not just anybody gets the chance to live so long.
至于这到底是机缘还是诅咒，恐怕只有时间能给出答案……
As for whether this is a blessing or a curse, I'm afraid only time can deliver an answer...
Doesn't seem that thrilling to me.
这么说也没错，毕竟解决问题的关键只是抛弃压舱物而已，听起来确实很简单。
Sure, when it's put like that. After all, problem-solving is really all about jettisoning the ballast, that's all. It sounds really simple.
但真到了那时候，并不是人人都有断尾逃生的魄力。而且我觉得，最刺激的部分在于逃出来之后……
But when the time comes, not everyone has the guts to sever their own tail to escape. And I think the most thrilling part of all is in the escaping...
跨越如此漫长的时间到底是机缘还是诅咒，恐怕只有时间本身能给出答案。
Whether skipping such a long passage of time is a blessing or a curse, I'm afraid only time itself can deliver an answer...
我知道这故事很难以置信，但宇宙中离谱的事情实在太多了…何况我们是无名客，无名客不能被现实束缚了思维！
I know this story is unbelievable, but so many extreme things happen out there in the universe every day. Besides, we the Nameless cannot limit ourselves to the boundaries of reality!
况且这到底是机缘亦或诅咒，还尚未可知呢…恐怕只有时间能给出答案了。
What's more, I have no idea right now whether this is a blessing or a curse... I'm afraid only time can deliver an answer.
Make it a little more romantic.
浪漫一点的…那就说说小行星带的事吧。
More romantic... Then let's talk about the incident in the asteroid belt.
有一次，我开着飞船在宇宙中闲逛，阴差阳错地来到了一个不存在于星图上的地方：一片遍布小行星的太空垃圾场。
One time, I was piloting a spaceship and roaming the stars, when I accidentally came to a place that wasn't on the astral charts: A space junkyard filled with asteroids.
这种疏漏往往是因为地图数据不足。只要把正确的坐标上报给公司，就可以拿到一大笔信用点奖励。
Such oversights are often due to insufficient map data. I could get huge credits rewards for reporting correct coordinates to IPC.
可惜我自己就在公司享有一笔悬赏，这钱怕是赚不到了——但不耽误我在周围逛一圈，万一能遇到什么宝贝呢？
It's a shame IPC had a bounty out on my head, so that money's out of my reach for now — but that doesn't stop me from taking a stroll around there. Who knows what treasure I might find?
这地方障碍物太多，舰载仪器除了发出恼人的警报外什么忙都帮不上。没办法，我只能用肉眼观察，手动操控飞船。

There were so many obstacles in this place that the ship's instruments kept tripping alarms everywhere, rendering it useless. I had no choice but to make observations by dead reckoning, and manually piloted the spaceship.
就在我聚精会神、眼观六路的时候，一个张牙舞爪的外星人忽然飘到了驾驶窗前……
Just as I was focusing all my attentions on the six directions around me, the snarling teeth and claws of this alien suddenly floated into sight...
是啊！不过我并没有被吓到，我的大心脏可是比钻石都坚固——即便这怪物长着两对眼睛，皮肤比伊须磨人都光滑，我内心也毫无惧意。
Yeah! But I wasn't scared. My heart is tougher than diamond — despite this monster having two pairs of eyes and skin smoother than an Insumousu local. There was no dread in my heart.
我仔细一看，发现那家伙身上的皮肤干瘪不堪，似乎已经死了…看它的样子，在宇宙中生存应该没什么问题才对。
Upon closer look, I realized that this guy's skin was all dried up as though dead... Judging by its appearance, it should have no problem whatsoever surviving in the universe.
两对眼睛、皮肤光滑，有点像长了手脚的鱼——看它的样子，在宇宙中生存也没什么问题。
Two pairs of eyes and smooth skin, almost like a fish with arms and legs — judging by its appearance, it should have no problem whatsoever surviving in the universe.
后来我仔细一看，才发现那家伙已经死去多时了。
Upon closer look, I realized that this guy was dead, and had been so for some time.
我仔细一看，才发现那家伙已经死了。它长得像是有手脚的鱼，外表似乎很适合在宇宙中生存。
Upon closer look, I realized that this guy was dead. It looked like a fish with arms and legs, and its exterior seemed as though it would have no problem surviving out in the universe.
我从没在智库里见过类似的生物，这意味着它的种族要么尚未被发现，要么…已经灭亡。
I've never seen anything like it in the data bank, which means it's either an undiscovered species, or... extinct.
后来我又对这片星区做了些勘探，发现那些小行星并非自然形成。它们是一场爆炸产生的碎片，原本来自同一颗星球。
Later on, I did some digging around this star system and discovered those asteroids were not formed naturally. They were fragments from an explosion, and were all sourced from the same planet.
计算机解析了样本成分，测得碎片诞生于十多个琥珀纪前——那个时期发生了一件大事，你应该知道。
The computer analyzed the sample composition, and found that the fragments were formed over <unbreak>10</unbreak> Amber Eras ago — a huge event happened back then, which you should know about.
是星核——「万界之癌」就是自那时开始侵害诸界的。
It was a stellaron — the Cancer of All Worlds that set out on its invasion of all worlds thereafter.
没错，就是从那时起，「万界之癌」开始侵害诸界。
That's right. It was from that moment forth that the Cancer of All Worlds started its invasion across all worlds.
毁于星核的文明并不少见。最初我也没把这事放在心上，打算上报完坐标就走——直到我发现了这些小行星表面的符号。

Civilizations destroyed by stellarons are not altogether uncommon. At first, I didn't really take it seriously, and was planning on warping out of there as soon as I'd reported the coordinates — that is, until I discovered those symbols on the surface of the asteroids.
那真是十分壮观的场面，每颗小行星上都如纹身般镌刻着密集的块状痕迹。这些痕迹有深有浅、形状各异，无疑是经由人力开凿出来的，仿佛…某种文字。

It really was a magnificent sight, with these blocks of dense scratches etched onto each asteroid like a tattoo. There were both deep and shallow markings, in all kinds of different shapes, which undoubtedly were carved out by someone as if... they were glyphs from some civilization.
这幅奇景勾起了我的兴趣——哪个无名客能拒绝探险的邀请呢？
The sight piqued my interest — which of the Nameless could ever refuse such an invitation to explore?
于是我继续向垃圾场中央行驶，一路上尽是大大小小的刻字石，以及…修建在小行星上的房子。
So I continued on into the center of the trash dump, passing blocks with glyphs carved into them along the way as well as... little houses built onto the asteroids.
这些房子看起来像铁皮拼成的蜂巢，结构简单但数量繁多，显然是人工建造的——这里曾经住着一整个聚落！
These houses looked like little honeycombs woven out of tin foil. Simple in structure and numbering in the hundreds, they were clearly artificially built — an entire settlement once lived here!
我挨个检查那些住房，见到了更多外星人的尸体。房间内完全没有生存物资，除了简单的石镐外也几乎没有工具。
I checked over the houses but only found more alien corpses. There were no supplies in any of the buildings, and almost no tools at all save for a crude pickax.
可以想象，他们是这颗星球的幸存者，不知用什么手段逃离了灭顶之灾，却注定因资源耗尽走向死亡。
It's possible that they are the survivors of this planet, escaping utter catastrophe through some means but doomed to ultimately die due to exhaustion of resources.
在生命的最后时刻到来前，他们将至关重要的信息——可能是史书，也可能是对星核的研究——以文字形式{RUBY_B#记忆}记录{RUBY_E#}在了这些小行星表面。

Before the final moments of their lives, they {RUBY_B#memories}recorded{RUBY_E#} vital information — maybe histories, or research into Stellarons — in the form of glyphs carved into the surfaces of these asteroids.
这一工作实在太繁琐，太漫长…究竟是怎样坚定的意志在支撑他们做完这一切？我不知道，但他们的成果无疑铸就了母星文明的丰碑。

This work looks truly tedious and boring... What kind of firm resolve supported their completion? I don't know, but their achievements have no doubt left a monument for their home planet and civilization.
可惜联觉信标不能解读文字，漫长的无规则运动也早已破坏了这些小行星的顺序，不然我一定要读一读，看看究竟是什么内容能让他们在死亡面前保持团结。

It's a shame that the Synesthesia Beacon could not decrypt text, and long years of irregular movements have long since destroyed the sequence these asteroids were supposed to be read in. Otherwise, I would have read it — to see what it was that kept them going as a team on the brink of death.
最后，我决定把坐标发送给公司——这会暴露我的行踪，而且捞不到半点好处。不过在如此壮观的景象面前，钱已经无足轻重了。

Finally, I decided to send in the coordinates to IPC — which would expose my location without offering me up any gain whatsoever. But after witnessing such a grand sight, money no longer seemed to matter.
应知晓的故事我已铭记，那些密文还是留给专业人士来伤脑筋吧。我相信他们比我更擅长解读。
I'd learned the lesson I was supposed to, and the secret of the glyphs should be left to professionals. I trust they will be better than me at decoding them.
Sounds like it'll become a tourist destination...
也许吧，但相比之下，我更不希望这里的故事被永远埋没。
Maybe. But in comparison, I'd rather this story wasn't buried forever.
…那样艰苦的努力却不能为人所知，未免太可惜了些。
...It would be a great pity for such hard work to go unrecognized.
So did you manage to escape?
当然，一脚油门的事而已。
Of course, just gotta put that pedal to the metal.
如果公司的效率真高到有了坐标就能立即出动，那他们早把我抓住了。
If IPC were ever powerful enough to send out an immediate dispatch for my coordinates, they'd have had me long ago.
话说回来，这个故事够浪漫吗？
Anyhow, was that story romantic enough for you?
There's a kind of majestic beauty to it.
是吧？相比于爱情，我更喜欢这种不寻常的浪漫。
Right? I prefer this kind of unusual romance over traditional love.
银河中有亿万颗恒星，在如此广大的尺度上，二人间的执念难免显得渺小。
There are hundreds of millions of stars in the cosmos. On such a vast scale, this mutual infatuation between two people seems inevitably insignificant.
总之，你能喜欢它真是再好不过了。
Anyway, it's great that you liked it.
唉，果然用言语描述出来还是差了些味道。
*sigh* So it seems like you lose some of the magic when you put it into words.
有机会的话，你真该亲眼见识下那副景象…也许可以把它做成梦泡？
If you get the chance, you really should try and see that sight with your own eyes... Maybe you could make it into a dream bubble?
哈哈，那确实也是一种浪漫，不过我更喜欢这种…不寻常的浪漫。
Haha, that's indeed one kind of romantic ideal, but I prefer this kind of... unusual romance.
毕竟，二人的执念只是银河中的万千联系之一。宇宙很精彩，不是吗？
After all, their infatuation is just one of uncountable connections throughout the cosmos. The universe is truly wonderful, isn't it?
嗯…一般来说，这片宇宙发生的一切都只是巧合的堆砌，人类很难从中学到什么道理。
Well... generally speaking, everything that happens in this universe is just a pile of coincidences stacked up, and it's difficult for humans to learn anything from that.
不过，我确实有个符合你要求的故事。
However, I do have a story that fits the bill.
大概十个琥珀纪前，星际间还流传着「机械海盗王」韦兰的臭名……
About <unbreak>10</unbreak> Amber Eras or so back, the notorious "Mechanical Pirate King" Wyland roamed the galaxies...
这位贼人年轻时几乎把自己身上的全部零件都换成了义体，在星区内兴风作乱，犯罪无数，头上顶着的悬赏足以买下一整颗星球——很难说其中有没有我的功劳。

When this pirate was young, he switched out almost all of his body parts with prosthetics, wreaking havoc throughout star zones and committing countless crimes. The bounty on his head was enough to buy a whole planet of your own — it's hard to say whether that included my contributions.
等年老后，韦兰的脑子已经被过多的改造压垮。疼痛与幻觉每天折磨着他，把他变成了狂躁易怒又神经兮兮的…怪物。
In his old age, Wyland's mind became overwhelmed by excessive mods. Pain and hallucinations tortured him daily, twisting him into a mad, irritable, neurotic... monster.
曾经忠诚的部下要么离开了他，要么被他残忍地折磨。就在海盗团即将分崩离析时，一位戴面具的访客找上韦兰，说虚空鲸的大脑提取液可以缓解这种不良反应。

His once-loyal crew either deserted him or were cruelly tortured by him. Just as this band of pirates was about to fall apart, a masked visitor came to Wyland telling him that an extract from the brain of the void song whale could relieve his adverse reactions.
这可不是什么灵药，这是陷阱。我极力反对这个提案，但走投无路的韦兰不会放过任何一根救命稻草。最后，我与他分道扬镳。

This wasn't some kind of cure-all; it was a trap. I strongly opposed this suggestion, but Wyland was at wits' end and could not help but clutch on to any last straw that might save his life. Finally, he and I took our separate ways.
而对虚空鲸的屠杀，也自此拉开序幕……
And so also began the massacre of the void song whales...
为保证猎杀效率，韦兰将自己的神经连进了舰桥终端，从而对整艘战舰如臂指使。代价就是，一旦失去虚空鲸提取液的供给，他就会在数小时内器官衰竭，最后死去。

Wyland hooked his nervous system straight into the bridge terminal for maximum hunting efficiency, so that commanding the entire battleship was like moving a limb. The cost was that once he lost his supply of whale extract, he would experience organ failure and perish soon after.
那是一段疯狂的日子，每天都有鲸群在炮火中丧生。就连「泯灭帮」都被韦兰的雄心折服，自愿加入这场狩猎——净瞎凑热闹。
Those were crazy days, with whales perishing in the cannon fire every day. Even the Annihilation Gang was won over by Wyland's ambition, willingly joining this hunt — just for the thrill of it.
多次警告无果后，星际和平公司找上了巡海游侠，提议联手铲除这个作恶已久的祸害。最后，双方各自派出精锐战力，计划对韦兰的旗舰发动一次斩首行动。

After multiple unheeded warnings, the Interastral Peace Corporation reached out to the Galaxy Rangers about building an alliance to wipe out this enduring scourge. In the end, both sides contributed crack troops and planned to launch an operation to lop the head off Wyland's main battleship.
谁说不是呢？但即便这样豪华的阵容， 也远远称不上胜券在握。
Who said it wasn't? But even with such a luxuriant lineup, victory was far from guaranteed.
不要以为斩首很容易。战舰的雷达就是韦兰的双眼，战舰的火炮则是他手臂的延伸。想要登上这样一艘怪物，没有过硬的驾驶技术可办不到。
Don't think a beheading operation is easy. The battleship's radar was Wyland's eyes. Its cannons were extensions of his arms. Boarding such a monster as this requires perfect mastery of piloting.
好在我也参与了那场战斗…以运输舰驾驶员的身份。
Luckily, I also participated in that battle... as the pilot of the transport vessel.
当我们打开舰长室的大门时，眼前只有空空如也的玻璃缸和杂乱丢弃的输液管——其中最大的那根链接着韦兰的后背……
When we opened the door to the captain's quarters, all we could see was an empty glass tank and scattered drips hanging about the place — the largest of which was hooked up to Wyland's back...
韦兰趴在地上，像条脱水的鱼一样，死了。
Wyland lay on the ground, like a fish out of water — dead.
不，那样就没有教育意义了。
No, that isn't really very educational.
当时我们也很疑惑。本以为会有一场惊世大战，没想到一弹未发就跳到了收尸阶段。
We were puzzled at the time too. We thought there would be this ferocious battle, but it shifted straight to the cleanup stage without a shot being fired.
后来我们得知：早在这场战斗发生前，韦兰就把自己手头的提取液全卖掉了，买家是一位出价颇高的面具人。
Later on, we learned that Wyland had sold off all the extract he had well before the battle to a masked buyer who paid very good money for it.
他原以为缸里的存货足够挺到下一次狩猎，结果找了几周都没发现猎物——根据博识学会的统计，那片星区早就没有活着的虚空鲸了。

He thought the supplies in the tank would last until the next hunt, but could find no prey after weeks of searching. According to the statistics from the Intelligentsia Guild, there haven't been any living void song whales in that sector for a long time.
所以，当联军打上门时，韦兰已经因义体的排异反应而死去多时……
So when the righteous alliance attacked, Wyland had already been dead for some time due to graft-host rejection...
直到生命的最后一刻，他还在试图用嘴去吸输液管，好弄出几滴残留的提取液来。
He was still trying to use his mouth to suck the last few drops of extract out of the tubes during his final moments.
是啊，也很有{RUBY_B#「欢愉」}乐子{RUBY_E#}。为祸一方的机械海盗王，最后落得个这样的死法。
Yes, truly an {RUBY_B#"Elation"}enjoyable{RUBY_E#} moment. The Mechanical Pirate King, a menace of the era, meeting his end like this.
相比之下，我还是更欣赏有肉时的他。
By comparison, I enjoyed him much more in the flesh.
最能打的韦兰死了，其他人自然作鸟兽散。不过公司和游侠还是让他们得到了应有的惩罚。
Wyland was the best fighter and died. The rest of them naturally scattered to the solar winds. But IPC and Rangers still meted out to them the punishment they deserved.
唉，有机体总是喜欢把自己改造成机械，韦兰也不例外……
*sigh* Organic creatures love modding themselves into robots. Wyland was no exception...
但他从来都没能摆脱人性中的贪欲，直到最后，滑稽地死在了管子拼成的王座里。
But he was never able to rid himself of human greed, until in the end he died farcically upon a throne made of twisted tubes.
…愚蠢又可恨的家伙，还不等找到宇宙秘宝就急着把自己弄死…这下是我输了。
...Foolish, hateful scoundrel. He rushed off to kill himself before he could find the secrets of the universe... This time, I lost.
关于他的死期，我曾经和他打过一瓶酒的赌。看来最后是他赢了。
I once made a bet with him about the day he'd die over a bottle of liquor. Seems like he won out in the end.
…可我却不知该怎么把奖品交给他。
...But I've got no idea how to give him his prize.
我感觉到…某些逝去的东西正重新回到我体内。
I feel like... certain things that are lost are returning to me.
愤怒？似乎不仅如此…还有一股力量，能驱使人前进的力量。
Is it anger? It's more than just that... There's a force, one that motivates.
怪不得自从来到这里后就总觉得少了点什么——情绪跟动力是息息相关的啊！
No wonder I've been feeling as though something's missing since I came here — emotion and motivation are intimately connected!
我想起了一些东西，关于我来到这里的初心，以及下一步要做的事……
I remembered why I came here in the first place, and what I needed to do next...
我曾经也是一个无名客——或许你已经猜到了。除了无名客，还有谁会闲到走遍宇宙呢？
I was once a Nameless, myself — as you no doubt have guessed. Who else could be free enough to roam around the universe but one of us?
不过我没有赶上列车开拓银轨的时代，只好加入了一个自负盈亏的民间组织。
However, I wasn't around for the dawn of the trailblazing star rail era, so I had to join a civil organization that was responsible for its own finances.
相比于探索未知的开拓者，那时的我更像是拿钱办事的佣兵，每天都和同伴为报酬的分配而争吵……
Compared to those Trailblazers exploring the vast unknown, I was more of a mercenary, getting paid to get things done. I'd argue with my companions daily about how we'd split the profits...
老实说，这与我想象中的开拓之旅相差甚远——我甚至都算不上真正的无名客。
If I'm being honest, this was a far cry from the trailblazing expedition I'd imagined — I can't even call myself a Nameless anymore, either.
于是在某个契机下，我脱离组织、成了独行侠。自那之后，我想去哪就去哪，想打谁就打谁，逍遥自在。
So when an opportunity arose, I left the organization and became this loner. Since then, I can go wherever I want, fight whomever I want, and everything feels so free and breezy.
不设限的旅行的确给我带来了许多见闻，但传说中众人齐心共进的景象，我终究是没有看到…也许那只存在于遥远的过去吧。

This free-roaming lifestyle brought me a wealth of experience, but I never got to see the legendary spectacle of a group of people working together as one... Maybe it only ever existed in the remote reaches of the past.
扯远了…我来匹诺康尼是为了会见一位同僚，一位隐藏身份的无名客——我们的老前辈「钟表匠」。
I digress... I came to Penacony to meet a colleague, a Nameless with a secret identity — our senior and mentor, the Watchmaker.
还没人知道钟表匠是谁吧。
Nobody knows who the Watchmaker is yet, right?
的确，但如果他是个无名客，一切就都说得通了。毕竟他留下了那样的遗产。
True, but that would make a lot of sense if he was one of the Nameless. I mean, he left behind that inheritance.
还没有…但你难道不觉得，如果他是无名客的话，一切就说得通了吗？毕竟他留下了那样的遗产。
Not yet... but don't you think that would make a lot of sense if he was one of the Nameless? I mean, he left behind that inheritance.
那可是吸引了全世界目光的遗产，仿佛宇宙尽头的秘宝！而想要得到这宝物，只需要踏上{RUBY_B#「开拓」}他曾经走过{RUBY_E#}的道路。

That's an inheritance that attracted attention from all over the world, like a secret treasure at the end of the universe! And to get it, you need to step on the path {RUBY_B#Trailblaze}that he once took{RUBY_E#}.
除了无名客，世上还有什么人能想出如此浪漫的点子？哪怕遗产只是个谎言，那也是留名青史的弥天大谎！

Who else in the world could ever come up with such a romantic idea, but one of the Nameless? Even if it's all a lie, it's still the kind of monstrous lie that can consign your name to the annals of history!
得知有这号人物存在时，我心底的某些火焰重新燃了起来。这才是无名客该有的样子！我非得和他碰一碰不可。
When I learned that this kind of person existed, some kind of fire was rekindled in my heart. This is what the Nameless should be like! I had to meet him in person.
…但谁知道要见他一面居然这么麻烦。你也读过他留下的谜语吧？真■■绕啊！
...But who knew it would be such a huge pain to meet him. You've read the riddle he left behind, right? So ■■■■■■■ puzzling!
好在科琳船长总是留有后招：来匹诺康尼之前，我曾联系到一位与家族关系匪浅的线人，让他帮我搞了些内部资料，也许有助于解开谜语。

Luckily Captain Colleen always has a backup plan: Before coming to Penacony, I got in touch with an informant who's tight with The Family, and asked him to get me some inside intel that might be useful when it comes to solving that riddle.
那位线人对我说，东西放在了<color=#f29e38ff>艾迪恩公园的垃圾桶</color>里。我本打算去取，但不知为何没有行动。
So my informant tells me, the things were placed in the <color=#f29e38ff>trashcan at Aideen Park</color>. I was meant to go and pick the stuff up, but for some reason I hadn't.
似乎是在梦里待久了的缘故，我逐渐变得枯燥、慵懒，对什么都提不起劲……
I think because I've been in a dream for so long, I've gradually grown bored and lazy. I can't seem to get excited about anything...
要说回到现实躲一躲吧…我那房间位于楼梯边上，吵得要死，对面还偏偏放了盆花——那味儿熏得我难受，一出门就打喷嚏。

And if I get back to reality to hide from this feeling... My hotel room's next to the staircase and it's plucking noisy. It so happens that there's a pot of flowers right outside — so pungent that it's hard to take. I'm sneezing despite being barely out the door.
总而言之，我度过了一段无比空虚的日子，每天浑浑噩噩不知干些什么。等回过神来，我已经上了贼船、成为筑梦师了……
So to summarize: I spent a while bored out of my skull, confused, not knowing what to do. When I come back to my senses, I find myself working for the baddies already as a Dreamweaver...
现在我意识到，不能再这样下去了。倘若一直不付诸行动，等我老死那天都见不到真正的无名客！
I now realize that I can't keep going on like this. If I don't take action, I won't be able to see the truest of the Nameless there ever was!
可筑梦边境人多眼杂，我亲自去实在太过显眼——我留着这个筑梦师的身份还有用呢。
But there are so many people at the Dream's Edge, it would be too conspicuous for me to visit myself — and this role as a Dreamweaver does have its perks.
所以我想请你帮忙，替我把那东西取来。
So I'd like your help in getting me that stuff.
如果你愿意帮忙，我可以把资料上的信息与你分享。哦，我还可以告诉你一些关于我自己的秘密……
If you're willing to help, I can share the info with you. Oh, and I can tell you a few secrets about myself, too...
考虑到你可能对这两样东西都不感兴趣…之后我再给你打一笔钱好了。
Considering how you might not be interested in either of these things... I can wire you some cash later.
漂亮！我就知道「无名客」是天底下最靠得住的一群人！
Sweet! I knew the Nameless are gonna be the most reliable people in the cosmos!
你考虑吧，我先默认你同意了。
You think it over. I'll just take it as your tacit agreement.
记住了，东西放在<color=#f29e38ff>艾迪恩公园的垃圾桶</color>里。
Remember: The things were placed in the <color=#f29e38ff>trashcan at Aideen Park</color>.
正当你打算离开时，一个人影拦在了你的面前.....
Just as you're about to leave, a figure blocks your way...
{M#小子}{F#丫头}，情况紧急…你赶快把东西交给我，具体的过后再解释。
{M#Boy}{F#Girl}, it's urgent... Please get me that stuff ASAP. I'll explain everything to you later.
No time to dwell on that right now!
现在没空交代来龙去脉了！
There's no time for the whole enchilada right now!
听着，我有一个预感，我们已经被……
Listen up. I've got a bad feeling that we've...
科琳…消失了？她在现实酒店里遇到了什么吗？
Colleen... disappeared? Did she encounter something inside the hotel in reality?
她好像说过自己的房间离楼梯很近，门口还摆着花…或许我该去看看。
I think she said her room was beside the stairwell, and there was a pot of flower by her door... Maybe I should take a look.
喂，别以为你有那装置就安全了，公司武装人员有的是办法进去。早点投案自首，对大家都好啊！
Hey, don't think you're safe just because you have that device on you. IPC guards have plenty of ways to get in. Surrender now, for the sake of everyone!
大哥，咱们就在门口等着，看那女人能嚣张到什么时候。
Dude, let's wait at the door and see how much that lady can keep up the smugness.
What are you two doing here?
这灰毛{M#小子}{F#丫头}又是从哪儿冒出来的？
Where did this gray-haired {M#boy}{F#girl} come from?
哎，对待游客就别那么粗鲁了嘛。
Hey, don't be so rude to the patrons.
我们两个是星际和平公司的员工，到匹诺康尼来度假旅游的。
We're staff from the IPC, and we're here in Penacony on vacation.
昨晚，我们和这房间里的女人打了个照面，发现她长得跟公司悬赏令上的逃犯有几分相似。
Last night, we met the woman in this room and discovered that she bore a striking resemblance to a fugitive on the IPC bounty hunt.
出于匹诺康尼的安全考虑，我们打算好好调查她一番，看看基因数据能不能对上号…结果才刚敲了下门，就被她在房门上安装的护盾给弹飞了。

For the sake of Penacony's safety, we planned to thoroughly investigate her and try to get a match from her genetic data... but as soon as we tried knocking on the door, we bounced off the repelling shield she'd installed.
哼，一看就是心里有鬼！她要真清白无辜，为什么不让我们进去？
Hmph, she's obviously guilty! If she really is innocent, then why wouldn't she let us in?
这里面一定有什么误会。
There has to be some kind of misunderstanding here.
误会？我只知道悬赏金没有误会！
Misunderstanding? All I know is that there's no misunderstanding about the bounty!
Perhaps you've got the wrong person?
哪怕认错了，她也必须出来接受调查！
Even so, she must still submit to our investigations!
谁都不会随便让人进房间吧…
Nobody would just let any stranger into their room...
说的也是…但这不能作为她拒绝接受调查的理由！
True... But that's no excuse for refusing to submit to an investigation!
倘若她真是通缉犯，我们兄弟俩就相当于帮匹诺康尼拆掉了一颗炸弹…顺道还能赚点外快；倘若她不是，那也不过虚惊一场。稳赚不赔的事啊！

If she really is a wanted suspect, then the two of us have pretty much defused a ticking time bomb for Penacony... while making a little bank on the side. And if she isn't, then it would all just be a false alarm. It's a guaranteed way to make a profit!
今天非得给她揪出来不可！
We'll flush her out today, for sure!
这件事交给我来解决吧。
You? What are you going to do about it?
在赏金交到手里之前，我们可不会轻易让出这个位置。
Before the bounty is in our hands, we won't just give up this position.
说到底，你们俩就是想要钱吧？
Speaking of which, you two want the money, right?
Money? Heh, you can say that again...
在赏金交到手里之前，我们可不会轻易让出这个位置。
Before the bounty is in our hands, we won't just give up this position.
你们凭什么这样强迫别人？！
What gives you the authority to force someone else like that!?
那你有更好的方案吗？拿出来！
Well, do you have a better idea? Show us!
在赏金交到手里之前，我们可不会轻易让出这个位置。
Before the bounty is in our hands, we won't just give up this position.
I'm interested in this person, too.
是吗？那我们算竞争关系了。到手的肥肉可不能随便让给你！
Really? Then we're competitors. I'm not gonna let you in on this cash cow that just fell into my lap!
咳咳！这个数倒是挺有诚意，但还不足以让我们放弃调查……
*coughs* This amount is not bad, but it's still not enough to make us give up the investigation...
每人一千信用点，我们就承诺不再追究此事，如何？
How about <unbreak>1,000</unbreak> credits each, and you promise to drop this entire thing?
You're frank, I like that. Show me the money.
数目确认无误…居然真能一下子掏出这么多钱来。
The amount is right... You have that much money on hand?
（小声）大哥，这家伙看上去来头不小，说不定是来度假的大人物。
(Whispering) Boss, this one looks like they might be somebody important. They might even be some bigwig here on vacation.
（小声）我知道，你没必要当着{M#他}{F#她}的面跟我说。
(Whispering) I know. There's no need for you to tell me in front of {M#him}{F#her}.
咳咳，既然钱已经付完了，那我们就走、走吧……
*coughs* Seeing as the money's been paid, let's go...
你们还跟我谈上条件了？！
You dare to negotiate with me!?
（小声）大哥，这个人气势如此凶悍，说不定有点来头。我们…还是不要硬碰硬为好。
(Whispering) Dude, this one's no pushover, {M#he}{F#she} might be someone important. Let's... not clash head-on.
（小声）我知道，你没必要当着{M#他}{F#她}的面跟我说。
(Whispering) I know. There's no need for you to tell me in front of {M#him}{F#her}.
咳，假如你坚持的话，每人五百也不是不行…所以，交钱吗？
*coughs* We are... amenable to the idea of getting <unbreak>500</unbreak> each, as goodwill. So... please pay up?
数目确认无误…那我们就走、走吧……
The amount is right... Let's g—go...
哎，你这答应的也太快了吧！
Hey, you agreed to that far too quickly!
咳…这个数字确实非常可观，我们乐意接受。但是，你真能拿出这么多钱来吗？
*coughs*... It's an impressive number, and we're happy to accept it. But can you really put together all of it?

（小声）大哥，这家伙看上去来头不小，说不定是来度假的大人物。

（小声）我知道，你没必要当着{M#他}{F#她}的面跟我说。
(Whispering) I know. There's no need for you to tell me in front of {M#him}{F#her}.
咳咳，既然钱已经付完了，那我们兄弟俩也不再掺和了。告辞！
*coughs* Seeing as the money's been paid, we shall take our leave. So long!
你们这样胡来，公司知道吗？
Would the IPC consider this an abuse of authority?
（小声）大哥，咱们这次是出来度假的，按理说确实没有权限让人接受调查。
(Whispering) Dude, we are here on vacation. Technically, we don't have the authority to force an investigation.
（小声）我知道，你不用当着{M#他}{F#她}的面特意跟我说一遍。
(Whispering) I know. You don't have to tell me specifically in front of {M#him}{F#her}.
咳咳，虽然这次的调查还没有上报本部，但公司肯定会对我们的行动表示支持……
*coughs* Although this investigation hasn't been reported to the HQ yet, the IPC will surely express support for our actions...
笑死，公司愿意得罪家族？
Hilarious! The IPC is willing to risk a fallout with The Family for this?
（小声）大哥，这家伙说的有道理，上头现在肯定不愿意跟家族撕破脸皮。
(Whispering) Dude, {M#he}{F#she} makes sense. The repercussions might be too risky for the higher-ups.
（小声）我知道，你能不能别再当着{M#他}{F#她}的面拆我台了？
(Whispering) I know. Can you please not undermine me again in front of {M#him}{F#her}?
我、我们的行为是符合公司价值观的！你个外人有什么权利这么说？
O—Our behavior is in line with IPC values! What right do you outsiders have to talk like that?
真出了事，你俩担得起责任？
If something really does happen, can you two take responsibility?
（小声）大哥，这家伙说的有道理。要是家族真和公司发生了什么矛盾，咱们承担不起风险啊。
(Whispering) Dude, {M#he}{F#she} makes sense. We can't afford the risk of being the cause of fallout between the IPC and The Family.
算了算了，这事我们兄弟俩不管了。走！
Fine, fine! Just forget about everything that happened here. We're leaving!
你们不走，我就打到你们走！
If you don't, I'll beat you up till you do!
好哇，敢这样跟星际和平公司的人说话，难道你没有领教过和平的铁拳？
Wow. If you dare take this tone with a member of the Interastral Peace Corporation, you must not have tasted the iron fist of peacekeeping.
这{M#小子}{F#丫头}如此嚣张，指不定身上也背着什么悬赏，咱们先把{M#他}{F#她}拿下再说。
If this {M#boy}{F#girl} can be so aggressive, there might be a bounty out for them. Let's arrest {M#him}{F#her} first and find out later.
说得对，今天咱们就大鱼小虾一锅端。弄{M#他}{F#她}！
You're right. We can put the champagne and lobster on ice for tonight. Get {M#him}{F#her}!
大、大哥，这小灰毛还真有两下子…现在怎么办？
B—Bro, this little gray-haired kid really knows {M#his}{F#her} stuff... What are we gonna do?
愿赌服输，挨打立正，看来咱们兄弟俩是没资格再插手此事了。走吧……
We played stupid games and won stupid prizes. Looks like this one's beyond our pay grade now. Let's get out of here...
（小声）大哥，这家伙看起来确实不太好惹…会不会是什么来度假的大人物？
(Whispers) Dude, this {M#guy}{F#gal} doesn't look like a walk in the park... Are they some kind of big shot here on vacation?
（小声）好汉不吃眼前亏…找个借口先溜吧。
(Whispers) A wise man knows better than to fight when the odds are stacked against him... Let's get away first.
咳咳…星际和平公司向来提倡以和为贵。经过短暂的组内探讨，我们俩一致决定让这件事翻篇。
*coughs* ...The IPC has always stood for keeping peace. After a short discussion among us, we have both decided to unanimously let this matter go.
不要以为我们怕了你…这只是出于大局考虑，各退一步而已！
But don't think that we're afraid of you... We're just taking into consideration the bigger picture and deciding to de-escalate!
我们走后，你可不许做什么危害星际和平的事！
We'll be keeping our eyes on you for when you endanger the interstellar peace!
看来你已经搞定他们了——我这边显示危险解除。{M#好小子}{F#好姑娘}，做的不错。
Looks like you've taken care of them — my side shows the danger has gone. Great job, {M#boy}{F#girl}, you've done well.
当然，一根头发都没伤到…不过必须承认，他们的突然袭击确实吓了我一跳。
Of course, not a hair on my head was harmed... but I have to admit, their sudden attack really caught me by surprise.
那两人为什么要纠缠你？
So why were those two bugging you then?
这就说来话长了…不得不说，他们的突然袭击确实吓了我一跳。
It's a really long story... but I have to admit, their sudden attack really caught me by surprise.
贿赂花的钱能报销一下吗？
Can you reimburse money spent on bribes?
没问题，科琳船长从不拿人手短。不得不说，他们的突然袭击确实吓了我一跳……
No problem. Captain Colleen never rips anyone off. I have to admit though, their sudden attack really caught me by surprise.
好在科琳船长总是留有后招：入梦之前，我在房门上架设了一台「锁闭式次元力场发生器」，将整个房间变得固若金汤——即使用激光炮轰也进不来！

Luckily Captain Colleen always has a backup plan: Before going to sleep, I set up my enclosed dimensional force field generator on the door so that the entire room would be impenetrable — not even under laser bombardment!
不过，如果他们一直在门外骚扰的话，我估计也没法安稳入梦…多亏你出手相助。
However, if they kept bugging me outside the door, I probably wouldn't be able to sleep well too... so thanks for your help.
既然事情已经解决了，我们还是回梦里继续聊吧。老地方，不见不散。
Now that everything's sorted, let's go back to our dreams and keep chatting. See you at the old place.
总算消停了…我要的东西还在吗？
Finally, it's stopped... Is that thing I want still there?
垃圾桶里只发现了这个。
That's all there was inside the trashcan.
That's not right. Let me see...
I can't read what's on it.
那应该是做过加密，让我看看……
It might be encrypted. Let me see...
接吻，洗澡，捉迷藏…这还真是花样繁多。
Kissing, washing, hide-and-seek... These sure are diverse.
…好，我已经把上面的文字还原了。按照之前的约定，我会给你一份复印件作为分享。
...Alright, I've restored the text on it. As agreed, I'll give you a copy to share.
When am I going to get paid?
别急，之后会付给你的。先听听我要说的秘密吧。
Relax. I'll pay you later. First, listen to the secret I'm about to tell you.
Why was the IPC bugging you?
好问题，我这就告诉你缘由。
Good question. Let me tell you why.
哎，别急，我还有秘密要讲，记得吗？
Ah, relax. I've still got secrets to tell you, remember?
我呢，之前是一个无名客，后来脱离团队单干，这些你都已经知道了——但我隐瞒了单干的内容。
You know, I used to be a Nameless as well, before leaving to strike out on my own. You know all of this — but I omitted the contents of my solo journey.
你可以猜猜看，这些年来我都在做些什么。
Take a guess at what I've been up to for those years.
打工是不可能打工的，何况是给公司这样的大企业。实际上，我还挺让他们头疼的……
Me, working for someone? Impossible, let alone for a large conglomerate like the IPC. In fact, I ended up giving them quite the headache...
After all, I am a pirate.
Did you go back home and work the land?
哈，那估计比现在遭罪多了。何况对无名客来说，安稳的日子未必就是好的。
Hah, that'd be a fate worse than now. Plus, a stable life doesn't suit a Nameless.
我虽不再是无名客，但骨子里依旧流淌着冒险的热血。
I may not be a Nameless any longer, but I've still got the spirit of adventure in my bones.
That's why I became a pirate.
You're not going to do something bad, are you?
坏事？也许吧，这取决于你对「坏」的定义。不过，大部分人应该都觉得我很坏吧……
Bad? Perhaps, but that depends on your definition of "bad." However, most people do seem to find me bad...
After all, I am a pirate.
在外面是，但对你我就说实话吧…那是个假身份。
To other people, sure, but I feel like telling you the truth... That's just a convenient disguise.
我打劫商船，走私矿物，有时也倒腾一些贵重玩意。但这都不是出于贪欲，而是为了自由地冒险…以及赚点小小的路费。
I've robbed trade vessels, smuggled minerals, and sometimes even peddled precious cargo. But I did it not out of greed, but for the freedom of wayfaring... and to make a little money for traveling.
星际和平公司在官网为我准备了一笔不多的悬赏，估计那俩二货就是为了这个来的…亏我最开始还以为是被什么大人物发现了。
The IPC put out a small bounty for me on its official site. I'm guessing those two fools came here just for that... I thought some big shot had discovered me right at the start.
别担心，我之前跟你说过的话都句句属实：我曾经是无名客，现在也依旧向往冒险。除了那笔悬赏以外，我和宇宙商人也没什么不同。
Don't worry. Everything I told you before is true: I was once a Nameless, and now I still yearn for adventure. I'm no different from any other traveling merchants, besides the bounty on my head.
在听说了匹诺康尼的消息后，我就知道我必须来这儿，无论付出什么代价……
As soon as I heard about Penacony, I knew that I had to come here. No matter the cost...
我是一个不幸的人，没能赶上英雄们开拓寰宇的时代。一直以来，我都对真正的无名客心怀羡慕与嫉妒……
I'm an unfortunate soul, who never got to see the age of heroes trailblazing the universe. I've always been envious and jealous of the real Nameless ones...
但我也是个幸运的人。这时代的故事尚未落幕，我还有机会参与其中，窥见冒险尽头的宇宙秘宝。

But I know that I'm also really lucky. The curtain has yet to fall on the story of this era, and I still have the chance to take part in it and catch a glimpse of the secret treasure of the universe waiting there in the end of the adventure.
这是我的「开拓」理念。我认为冒险的尽头存在宝藏，能够回报一路付出的所有汗水。
This is my idea of Trailblaze. I think that there is some kind of precious treasure that exists at the end of all adventure that pays off all the effort you expend along the way.
倘若努力最后什么都得不到，人们前赴后继地探索还有什么意义呢？
What's the point of continuous exploration if you know you're not going to get anything in the end?
探索本身就是最大的意义。
Exploration is the main reward, in and of itself.
…很有无名客风格的回答。你身上依旧保留着理想，我不讨厌。
...That's a very Nameless answer from you. You still retain your ideals; that's okay.
虽然我的理想已经不再闪亮，但我还是希望理想的行为能得到回报。
Despite my ideals being far less glittery now, I still hope that ideal behavior can be rewarded.
Sounds way too focused on material gains...
的确，但满足功利心也是很重要的。
Sure, but it's vital to feel rewarded materially.
当我们把什么东西奉为圭臬的时候，世界就会往我们所追捧的那个方向发展。如今的人们选择钱——长久以来的人们都选择了钱。
When we regard something as a guiding principle, the world will develop in whatever direction we pursue. People today tend to choose money — as they have for a long time.
在这种眼光下，还有什么比宇宙尽头的秘宝更能激起人的探索欲呢？
From this perspective, what could stimulate people's desire for exploration more than a secret treasure at the edge of the universe?
等解开了钟表匠的秘密，我就要踏上自己的冒险了。这可能是我人生中最后一场冒险。
Once I've solved the Watchmaker's riddle, I'm going to embark on an adventure of my own. And it could just be the final adventure of my life.
我要去寻找他——不是为了钱，只是想站到他的面前，看看所谓的传奇人物到底有怎样耀眼的光芒，会不会把我这阴沟里的老鼠融化。
I'm going to look for him — not for money, but to stand before him and see how dazzling the so-called legend really is, and whether it's strong enough to melt even a rat in the gutter like myself.
还有那些对我做手脚、以为能用下三滥手段阻拦我的家伙…管它是家族还是什么公司，我总有一天要让他们付出代价！
And those guys who tried to sabotage me, thinking they could stop me by using dirty tricks... whether it's The Family or the IPC, one day, I'll make them pay!
<size=28>在我面前，哪怕宇宙规律都得让路！</size>
<size=28>Even the maxims of the universe shall step aside before me!</size>
哦对了，这是承诺给你的报酬，拿好。无名客也得食人间烟火啊。
Oh, by the way — here's the reward I promised. Hold onto that. Even a Nameless has to eat, just like the rest of the mortals.
一切结束之后，我们在冒险的尽头再会吧。
When everything is done and dusted, let's meet up again at the end of the adventure.
啊嘿！我的同僚，这次有什么打算吗？
Oh, my friend, what's the plan this time?
嘶~我冷静的头脑又重新占据了高地…最近的情绪可真多变。
Hiss... My logical mind has regained control... My mood has been all over the place lately.
据说梦境原本并非我们见到的样子，是家族将它塑造成了如今的模样……
Rumor has it, that dreamscape isn't always like this. It's The Family that molded it to its current state...
…如果我们的情绪也是受人操控的，可以像泥巴一样被肆意捏弄…岂不是会很可怕？
...If our emotions are controlled by others and could be manipulated like clay... wouldn't that be terrible?
科琳船长眉头微皱，怒意在她表面之下如暗流般涌动。
Captain Colleen frowned, her rage bubbling up beneath the surface like an undercurrent.
啧，突然想起一件烦心事……
Tsk, I suddenly remember something pretty distressing...
之前说过，我曾跟随民间无名客旅行过一段时间，后来分道扬镳了。这件事就是导火索。
As I said before, I traveled with some of the Nameless for a while, before we parted ways. This incident was the catalyst.
那天，我们的飞船在一颗沙漠星球补给物资。仓管从当地人手里买来了一头名叫大毛的生物——那是它的名字。至于它究竟是什么物种，我不知道。
That day, our spaceship was restocking provisions on a desert planet. The warehouse manager bought a creature named Hairy from the locals — that was its name. I don't know what species it was.
船员们看着这头长相酷似黄石牛的生物，决定把它煮了吃掉，我没意见。但就在厨师准备下刀时，大毛开口说话了……
The crew looked at this yellow boulder calf lookalike and decided to cook it up to eat. I had no objections. But just when the chef was about to stick the knife, Hairy spoke...
它说自己是一个智慧生物，希望能与我们讨论哲学和艺术，而不是被切成零散的肉块——是它主动向当地人要求上船的。

It said that it's an intelligent species and hoped to discuss philosophy and the arts with us — instead of being carved up into pieces of meat. It also said, it was its own idea to join everyone on the ship.
作为最见多识广的成员，我主动接下了与这「怪物」沟通的任务。我们从历史文化聊到星神崇拜，它的知识甚至比我都丰富。

As the most experienced crew member, I took charge of communicating with this "monster." We chatted about everything, from history and culture to Aeon worship — and its knowledge was even richer than mine.
于是我对同伴说，我们应该把它当成智慧生物、当作有思想的人来对待。大家都觉得我不可理喻。
So I told my companions that we should view it as an intelligent species and a sentient person. Everyone thought that I was being unreasonable.
厨师说：「我们还是决定把它吃掉。既然这玩意跟黄石牛长得差不多，肉质估计也差不到哪儿去」。
The chef said, "We still want to eat it. Since it looks similar to yellow boulder calves, the meat should taste the same."
那时我们的食物足够饱腹，这些人只是想尝尝异星生物的味道罢了…屠刀落下前，我看见大毛湿润的眼眶流出了泪……

At the time, we already had enough food to feed ourselves — but these people just wanted to try a new exotic species... Before the butcher's knife fell, I could see tears welling up in Hairy's eyes...
当天晚上，每个人都分到了一块多汁的肉排。大家对它的味道赞叹不已，但我一口也没吃。
Later that night, everyone enjoyed their own juicy cutlet. Everyone raved about how delicious it tasted, but I didn't take a single bite.
这次航行结束后，我就脱队单干了。
After this voyage was over, I left the team and struck out on my own.
因为我感到愤怒，也可以说是我的矫情心在作祟。
Because I was enraged. You could also say my impetuous nature played a role.
我不反对吃肉，但作为无名客，这些人明显缺乏对未知的敬畏和探索精神——他们应该加入泯灭帮才对……
I'm not against eating meat, but as the Nameless, these people obviously lack the awe for the unknown and the spirit of adventure — they should've joined the Annihilation Gang, instead...
大毛和我们一样，是拥有高级智慧的生物…我知道这说法有些奇怪，可当一头牛开始谈论灵魂与哲学时，真有人能把它单纯当作肉来看待吗？

Just like us, Hairy was a creature of high intelligence... I know it's strange to say, but when a cow starts talking about spirits and philosophy with you, how can anyone treat it as just a piece of meat?
你认为他们缺少同情心？
Do you think they lack any empathy?
不止，他们还缺少对未知事物的敬畏，将美妙的可能性扼杀在了摇篮中。
Not only that, they also lack an awe for the unknown — killing the wonder of possibility in the cradle.
大毛和我们一样，是拥有高级智慧的生物…我知道这说法有些奇怪，可当一头牛开始谈论灵魂与哲学时，真有人能把它单纯当作肉来看待吗？

Just like us, Hairy was a creature of high intelligence... I know it's strange to say, but when a cow starts talking about spirits and philosophy with you, how can anyone treat it as just a piece of meat?
I bet it tasted really good though...
如果你嘴馋，可以去餐馆里点上一份牛排，享受肉用牲畜给你带来的快感。
If you're a glutton, go to a restaurant and order a steak if you'd like to enjoy the meaty pleasures of livestock.
但大毛不是牲畜。它和我们一样，是拥有高级智慧的生物…我知道这说法有些奇怪，可当一头牛开始谈论灵魂与哲学时，真有人能把它单纯当作肉来看待吗？

But Hairy was no livestock. Just like us, Hairy was a creature of high intelligence... I know it's strange to say, but when a cow starts talking about spirits and philosophy with you, how can anyone treat it as just a piece of meat?
啧，突然想起一件烦心事……
Tsk, I suddenly remember something pretty distressing...

But Hairy was no livestock. Just like us, Hairy was a creature of high intelligence... I know it's strange to say, but when a cow starts talking about spirits and philosophy with you, how can anyone treat it as just a piece of meat?
从这件事中我学到了教训：有些人脑子里只能装下最低贱的欲望，不要试图让他们理解你。
I learned a lesson from all this: Some people can only entertain the most vulgar of desires. Never try reasoning with them.
现在我每次出发前都会带上超多粮食——只有让人饱腹到看见肉就反胃，他们才不会见什么都想尝尝味道。
Now I make sure that I pack plenty of extra rations whenever I head out. If people are full when they see meat, they won't be compelled to try and eat everything they see.
分给你一些吧，希望你永远不要被肚子影响判断。
Let me share a little with you. May your stomach never impact your decision-making ability.
科琳船长面色沉重地凝望着天空——又或是比天空更高的什么东西。
Captain Colleen stares up at the sky with a sullen face — or perhaps at something even higher than the sky.
唉，天堂的希冀，地狱的威胁，唯有一事是真——此生飞逝啊。
*sigh* The hopes of heaven, the threats of hell, but only one thing can be certain: Life is truly fleeting.
Why are you waxing poetic all of a sudden?
Did you just think of something?
我想起了一个被毁灭的星球。直到最后一刻降临前，那里的人们仍在挣扎着求生…至少大部分人如此。
I was just reminded of a destroyed planet. Until its very last moments, there were still people there struggling to survive... at least, most of them.
那颗星球名叫奥特兰，位于遥远偏僻的边境星系。它所属的恒星被一颗星核污染，不停释放出辐射。
That planet was called Outerland, and it was located in a distant border galaxy. It belonged to a star corrupted by a Stellaron, and was constantly releasing radiation.
这种辐射对植物没什么影响，却杀死了奥特兰上绝大多数动物，包括人类。
This radiation had little effect on plants, but was fatal to most of the animals on Outerland — including the people.
为了应对辐射，当地的筑城者在仅存的定居点上建起屏障，将内与外永远隔绝——光射不进屏障，人们只能在黑暗中苟且偷生。

To deal with the radiation, the local architects erected barriers around the remaining settlements to permanently separate the inside from the outside. No light could penetrate the barrier, and the people could only eke out a miserable existence in darkness.
的确，这个计划实行之初，不少人都提出了反对意见……
Sure is. When this plan was first raised, it was met with many an objection...
但在死亡的威胁面前，他们别无选择。
But faced with the threat of death, there were no other choices available.
At least they managed to survive.
是啊，最开始许多人都这么认为。但时间一久，再坚定的意志也注定被消磨……
Yeah, that's what people thought at first. But as time goes by, your resolve is bound to be ground away no matter how strong it may be...
沐浴在阳光下的人们，无法想象黑夜的难耐。
People used to bathing in the sun cannot fathom the unbearable darkness of the night.
雅利洛…哦，是你说过的那颗冰封星球。
Jarilo... Oh, the frozen planet that you mentioned before.
确有相似之处，但二者的结局则大相径庭……
They have their similarities, but their conclusions could not be further apart...
漫长的黑暗为奥特兰带来了安全，却也足以把人逼疯……
The long darkness brought security to Outerland, but also madness...
屏障内部的生活远比想象中艰难。无论是维持照明的能源，还是生存所需的物资，都无法均分给所有人。
Life inside the barrier was far harder than was envisaged. Neither the energy needed to keep the lights on nor the resources needed for survival could be distributed fairly to everyone.
在复杂的利益计算后，统治者下令：一切资源优先提供给护盾发射器「天幕塔」，确保屏障正常运作。未经允许，普通居民不能私自架设照明工具。

After complicated profit calculations, their leader ordered that all resources should be seized for the shield-projector Canopy Tower to guarantee normal operations for the barrier. Regular citizens could not set up lights without obtaining prior authorization.
越向城中央的天幕塔靠拢，所能分到的光照就越充足。这座高塔变成了压迫与不公的象征。
The closer you got to the Canopy Tower in the middle of town, the more light you could get. This tower became a symbol of oppression and injustice.
这些措施真能保护奥特兰吗？
Would these measures really be enough to protect Outerland?
确实保护了一段时间，说长不长、说短也不短…大概半个琥珀纪吧。
They were enough for a while. I can't say whether it was a long time or a short time... but it was about half an Amber Era.
也许吧，奥特兰确实因此存续了一段时间…大概半个琥珀纪吧。
Perhaps. Outerland did survive for some time because of it... for about half an Amber Era.
总之，这法子确实保住了奥特兰一段时间…大概半个琥珀纪吧。
In any case, this method has indeed preserved Outerland for a period... about half an Amber Era.
在这半个琥珀纪里，人们的希望逐渐转为不安——因为这种生活似乎永远没有尽头。
Hopes have dwindled during that time — because that kind of life never seemed to end.
恐惧和愤怒开始蔓延。有人说，我们不能一辈子都关在笼子里；也有人说，中心区的权贵应当多分出些资源来……
Fear and anger began to spread. Some protested, not wanting to spend their lives in a cage, while others said that the rich and powerful ones in the center should distribute more resources...
直到某天，一位老人在屏障边缘的贫困区架起大灯，宣告对天幕塔的反抗。奥特兰随即爆发了漫长且激烈的内战。

Until one day, an elderly man set up a spotlight in the poverty district at the edge of the barrier and revolted against the Canopy Tower. Thereafter, a long and bitter civil war broke out in Outerland.
混乱摧毁了天幕塔的能源核心。天空被撕开一个大洞，强辐射的阳光倾泻而入……
Chaos destroyed the energy core of the Canopy Tower. With a gigantic hole torn in the sky, the intensely radioactive sunlight poured in...
在生命的倒计时中，所有人再次看到了蓝天。
As they counted down towards their collective death, all the people glimpsed the blue sky once again.
那位老人则抱着一盆重新抽枝的花，喜极而泣——那是他亡妻留下的「三日卷柏」，只要一点阳光就能起死回生。

The elderly man cried tears of joy as he clutched a pot of flowers, its branch sprouting anew — it was the three-day spikemoss left by his late wife, which could be brought back to life with only a little sunlight.
他因一己私欲害死了所有人。
He got everyone killed because of his selfishness.
是吗？如果我是他，我大概会做出一样的选择。
Is that so? If I were him, I would probably do the same.
如果权贵让步，结局会好吗？
Will the ending be better if the rich and the powerful relented?
很难说。不公固然是个问题，但不是奥特兰最大的问题。
That's hard to say. Injustice was certainly a problem, but it wasn't Outerland's biggest issue.
How did you know about that?
不是只有亲临者才能讲故事，否则世界上就没几个人写书了。
No one says that only survivors can narrate their stories. Otherwise, there won't be many storytellers left.
至于我具体怎么知道的，你只能猜猜看咯——有秘密的女人才更有味道嘛。
As for how I know, feel free to hazard a guess — after all, no intriguing women go without secrets.
还是说回奥特兰吧，这件事让我感慨良多……
Let's go back to Outerland. I'm rather affected by this incident...
很难评价对吧？宇宙中就是会有这样的事情……
Hard to take a stand? Well, such things are common in the universe...
为了幸福生活，人们可以任劳任怨地付出。可如果最好的结局也只是生存——环境恶劣、神经紧绷、看不见半点好转的希望，那苦难似乎就不是值得忍受的东西了。

Humans will labor ceaselessly with no complaints for the sole purpose of living in happiness. However, if survival is the best outcome they can possibly reach — with a harsh environment, intensive mental stress, and absolutely no hopes of the situation ever improving — then the idea of enduring present sufferings for a better future would suddenly seem a lot less appealing.
可惜，即使这样差劲的生存，也不是那么容易就能得到……
Unfortunately, even such a terrible life is something that many people have to fight to achieve...
或早或晚，奥特兰终会灭亡，就像一场无法治愈的慢性疾病…除非有人修复被星核污染的太阳，但那又谈何容易呢？
It's only a matter of time before Outerland perishes, like a terminal chronic illness... Unless someone can fix its star contaminated by the Stellaron, but what are the chances of that?
我原本想把这故事做进梦境里，好让人们知道宇宙的另一头发生过什么。但家族说题材太过压抑，不予批准……
I wanted to make this story into the Dreamscape to educate people about what happened on the other end of the universe, but The Family found this topic too oppressive and did not approve it...
唉，真可惜，没有小小的悲剧作衬托，美梦又怎能凸显价值呢？
*sigh* What a pity. Without a little melancholy, how can we bring out the happiness in better dreams?
这个故事我已经听过了。
I have heard of this story.
噗嗤…不管往哪儿走，你都是在向前啊！
Pfft... No matter where you're heading, you're walking forward!
我突然想起了在新伦蒂尼恩的经历…正好心情不错，把它当个乐子讲给你听吧。
I suddenly recalled the New Londinium experience... Since I'm in a good mood, let me share it with you.
新伦蒂尼恩是公司旗下的工业星球，污染排放严重；空气里经常泛着一股呛人的烟味儿，就像在柜子里放了六个月的巴维鲁干酪一样臭。

New Londinium is IPC's industrial planet. A place that produces heavy pollution constantly. The air used to smell of suffocating smoke, stinking like Baviru cheese that had been sitting in the cupboard for six months.
我第一次去时没有经验，一下船就呼吸不畅，差点窒息…幸好路人及时找来了医生。
When I was there the first time, I was inexperienced and couldn't breathe when I got off the ship... Luckily, a passerby managed to bring me to a physician in time.
这个星球上的医生都戴着覆面的鸟嘴面具。我最开始还以为是故意凹造型的样子货，在内心狠狠嘲笑了一番。
All the physicians on that planet wore face-covering beak masks. Initially, I thought they had deliberately dressed themselves to look stylish and were silently mocking them.
直到他为我也戴上一副面具，我才感觉生锈的肺又活了过来——这玩意是公司推出的空气过滤器！在那么肮脏的环境里，没有这个还真是寸步难行。

It wasn't until they put the same mask on me that I realized I could breathe freely again... That was an air purifier mask made by the IPC! It was impossible to get around without that in the filthy environment.
就在这时，天空突然播放起公司的广告投影来。画面上的家伙身穿西装，用那种非常符合刻板印象的语气开口说道……
Just then, a projection of the company's advertisement appeared in the sky. In it was a man dressed in a suit, and he spoke in a tone that fit his appearance...
「新伦蒂尼恩的居民们，过去一年来大家都做得不错，公司效益取得了显著提高，环保工作也向前迈了一大步。」
"Citizens of New Londinium, all of you have been performing well for the past year. IPC's efficiency has also improved tremendously, a big step forward for environmental protection."
这话真令人绷不住，我当场就笑了出来，身边人对我露出看怪物的表情。
Those words were so ridiculous that I burst out laughing, and the people around looked at me like a freak.
于是我连忙解释说：这是人类历史上最古老的笑话之一——不管往哪儿走，你都是在向前啊！
So I hurried and explained to them that this was the oldest joke in the history of mankind — no matter where you're heading, you're walking forward!
我突然想起了在新伦蒂尼恩的经历…正好心情不错，把它当个乐子讲给你听吧。
I suddenly recalled the New Londinium experience... Since I'm in a good mood, let me share it with you.
